title: Bank Apps Showed Wrong Transactions
summary: Some people using apps from Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland saw other people's bank transactions on their phones. This was a mistake called a glitch. They could see things like payments from pubs or wages from jobs. One woman saw six different people's accounts, including their National Insurance numbers, which are important for benefits. The banks said sorry and fixed the problem. It is not clear how many people were affected. This has happened before with these banks. The apps had issues for a few hours in the morning, and a website called Downdetector.com showed many reports. People are worried because they saw private information like where others work or live.

special numbers used for benefits and jobs in the UK
programs on phones or computers for doing things like banking

background_read: Banks like Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland have apps for customers to check their money and make payments. Sometimes, computer problems called glitches can happen, causing mistakes. In this case, the apps showed other people's private information, which is a big problem because it can lead to identity theft or fraud. Banks need to keep information safe to protect customers. This incident shows why it is important for companies to test their apps carefully and fix errors quickly.
Article_Structure: WHO: Customers of Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland banks
WHAT: A glitch in bank apps showed other users' transactions and private information
WHERE: In the UK, affecting apps on phones
WHY: Due to a computer error that the banks have now fixed

perspectives.description: People are worried about privacy and security when their personal information is exposed.
